title: The Waiting Game ⏳
description: Why apps freeze, and how Dart prevents it with the Event Loop.

slides.title: The Frozen App 🥶
slides.content:
# Why Apps Crash

Usually, code executes Line 1, then Line 2, then Line 3 instantly.
But what if Line 2 says: `String data = downloadFromInternet()`;

Downloading takes 3 full seconds. If Dart waits on Line 2... your entire app freezes. The user can't tap buttons, scrolling snaps, and the OS might forcefully close the app for being 'Unresponsive'.

slides.title: Task Delegation 👨‍💼
slides.content:
## Asynchronous Programming

Dart runs on a **Single Thread** (one worker doing all the math).
To prevent freezing, Dart uses **Asynchronous** programming. 

Instead of standing there staring at the internet download, Dart shoves the download task into the back corner (the Event Loop), and instantly moves on to Line 3!

It handles UI clicks and math normally, and when the download finally finishes 3 seconds later, it raises its hand and Dart deals with it.

quizData.question: What is the primary goal of Asynchronous programming?

options.text: To make calculations mathematically more accurate.
To prevent long-running tasks (like network calls) from completely freezing the main thread and User Interface.

quizData.explanation: Async code delegates slow tasks so the main thread can keep painting the screen 60 times a second.
